content.rendered:
Teaching Philosophy 

As a multilingual who has lived and worked in three regions of the world, I believe that understanding and communication are the basis of how every living creature relates to their world. The most sacred duty of TESOL educators is to ensure that their learners are able to communicate in English and understand the respective cultures attached to the languages, to the best of their ability. It is the educators’ greatest responsibility to create a safe and nurturing learning environment, as well as provide engaging, inspiring, and relevant teaching material, for the learners to be able to fully be and express themselves, grow in all possible ways, with the goal of reaching and even surpass all their aspirations. Mastering the language, not just for academic pursuits, but the life-skills acquired through the education help in achieving a successful existence, so the learners can be a positive contribution to society and the human race. 

In this uncharted post-pandemic era, which might forever change the way we live, my focus in creating and offering virtual reality classrooms that are fully immersive, interactive, and live in real-time to schools, teachers, and students allowing unparalleled health safety than in-person classrooms. These virtual reality learning environments are remote learning without any discrimination nor compromise on face-to-face interaction with the instructor and socially between the students in a class. The classroom environment is “virtually ” limitless – from recreations of real places to the fantastic imaginary, on planet Earth or outer space, teachers and students are no longer bound to physical reality and can even co-create VR classrooms to explore together. 

The AltspaceVR platform where the classrooms I create are hosted is owned by Microsoft and is available to users and content creators free of charge, accessible through just a computer in 2D or a VR headset for full immersion in 3D. The social media platform hosts all sorts of events, meetups, and social spaces made by other content creators around the globe, essentially giving users a whole new community-based universe to learn, socialize and revel in. Users are also protected by the community standards of common online regulation. Even though the avatars are cartoon-like, there are tools such as “report”, “block” and “personal bubble” to ward off unwanted intrusions. The avatars are generated from a preset selection of features, supplying an overall similar look to all the very likable avatars personalized by each user. This universe furnishes ESL learners unlimited opportunities to practice the language learned, with a welcoming and friendly international community at any time on-demand. This is particularly useful for countries that do not speak English as their native or second language, therefore do not have a readily-available immersive target language environment in actuality. 

The teaching material is designed in a way that my students don’t just “receive” an education from me, but are empowered to co-create their lessons with me. This student-centered strategy gives learners ownership of their education and keeps the material truly relevant. If a selection of the lesson topics is required, then fairness can be illustrated through a democratic voting process. I would simply guide and assist the students in what they choose to learn. On subject topics delivery, lessons are scaffolded and build on the knowledge from one to the other, so the learners can possess a solid foundation from which to produce from. Low-stress informal assessment activities are dotted throughout each class, first in speaking, then in reading and writing, which guarantees that each student receives the individual attention needed. A variety of different teaching methods used appeals to the different intelligences of each learner, warrants equal opportunity to every student. 

In a mutually respectful, caring and supportive relationship, the teacher and students learn from and teach each other. Our classroom becomes an incubator for an idealistic model society, so we can realize our highest expectations back into the larger world. The methods and strategies I learned from my TESOL certification program at the prestigious University of California Los Angeles, demonstrated through the teachings of my mentor, Professor Kris Lambert, help students learn quickly and efficiently. The education experience with me is always enjoyable, where the students can reach their fullest potential while having fun – what I have concluded is the most effective way to learn. The cartoon-like VR classrooms cater to this end, loved by younger learners and adults alike. The customizable avatars also help offer a solution to TESOL teachers of color, who may face the challenge of preferential hiring practices for Caucasian teachers in regions such as Taiwan and Mainland China. Schools in those regions hire Caucasian teachers as a priority, to help learners practice overcoming their fear or shyness to speak to others that appear foreign to them. In the future, I aim to establish a school, to foster a community of institutions, educators, learners, and content creators worldwide to co-create and benefit from these fully immersive and interactive VR classroom experiences live and in VRealTime.

So fun!

I love the colors written on the flag. Great way to make connections. Super clear intonation. Great gestures! Lots of repetition! What a fun way to learn! Fantastic interaction, repetition and visual connections. This is simple yet really holds the learners’ attention. This is outstanding!!!

– Kris Lambert

Instructor

UCLA Extension TESOL Program
